items.title: Alphabet’s record-breaking $85B raise for Google’s AI business is a helluva good signal
items.raw_content: If Alphabet's record-breaking $85 billion stock sale signals investor appetite for AI-related offerings, we can see that investors are ready to chow.
items.summary: If Alphabet's record-breaking $85 billion stock sale signals investor appetite for AI-related offerings, we can see that investors are ready to chow.

items.title: Microsoft and OpenAI broke up — now they’re ready to fight
items.raw_content: At Microsoft's annual Build conference on Tuesday, the company announced a slew of new or expanded AI initiatives, including a super app, in-house reasoning models, a cybersecurity tool, and OpenClaw-esque AI agents. All this news added up to a clear message: Microsoft is positioned to be one of the biggest players in AI, and it's […]

items.title: Lovable signs multiyear deal with Google Cloud to up usage 5x, source says
items.raw_content: Lovable and Google signed an expanded multiyear deal that involves a 5x expansion of Lovable's footprint on Google Cloud, and expanded access to Anthropic Claude.
items.summary: Lovable and Google signed an expanded multiyear deal that involves a 5x expansion of Lovable's footprint on Google Cloud, and expanded access to Anthropic Claude.

items.title: Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents
items.raw_content: Coralogix is among a growing number of infrastructure firms betting that as AI systems move into production, demand will rise for tools that can monitor their behavior, troubleshoot failures, and provide the operational data needed to keep them running reliably.

items.title: As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise
items.raw_content: This week we've got tandem hands-ons with Google's new Gemini AI agent - Spark - from my colleagues David Pierce and Jay Peters. Their takeaways are similar: It's so effective that it's scary. Spark knew that David's dog is named Frida and knew the first name of Jay's wife, even though neither of them explicitly […]

items.title: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
items.raw_content: WhatsApp will charge businesses for using its AI agent based on token usage.
items.summary: WhatsApp will charge businesses for using its AI agent based on token usage.

items.title: Google’s Dreambeans, its weirdest-named AI tool to date, will turn your life into a cartoon
items.raw_content: Dreambeans is a curated list of AI-illustrated "stories" culled from the personal data in your Google account.
items.summary: Dreambeans is a curated list of AI-illustrated "stories" culled from the personal data in your Google account.

items.title: Amazon will show AI product images when you search for some reason
items.raw_content: Amazon will use visual search and AI to show AI-generated product images that match your search queries. The retailer says it will help guide users to products.
items.summary: Amazon will use visual search and AI to show AI-generated product images that match your search queries. The retailer says it will help guide users to products.

items.title: These two founders left Goldman and Meta to build voice AI for markets everyone else overlooked
items.raw_content: The startup's own stack for Africa and Middle East is now handling more than 17,000 calls per day.
items.summary: The startup's own stack for Africa and Middle East is now handling more than 17,000 calls per day.

items.title: Publishers will be able to opt out of AI Search, thanks to new regulation
items.raw_content: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features. The option will be tested in the U.K. then rolled out globally.
items.summary: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features. The option will be tested in the U.K. then rolled out globally.

items.title: Amazon’s search bar will invent AI-generated products you can’t buy
items.raw_content: Amazon's updated search bar will now show you AI-generated images of products as you describe them. For now, the in-app feature only surfaces AI images of clothing and home goods, allowing you to tap on the image that best matches what you're looking for and search for similar-looking items. In a blog post, Amazon positions […]

items.title: AI has a water problem — Google thinks it has a fix
items.raw_content: In the face of widespread backlash to the AI data center buildout throughout the US, Google is touting its efforts to minimize the environmental impact by actually increasing water for local communities. The company laid out five commitments around water use in a new blog post published Wednesday, including a goal to replenish more water […]

items.title: Google must let publishers opt out of AI Search features, rules UK
items.raw_content: Online publishers are getting more control over whether their websites appear in Google's AI Search features, thanks to a UK regulatory ruling. The new conduct rule imposed by the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) requires Google to let website owners keep their content out of features like AI Overviews and prevent it from being used […]

items.title: Microsoft’s first advanced reasoning AI is here
items.raw_content: Microsoft announced a bunch of new in-house AI models at Build 2026, including a new "flagship" model: MAI-Thinking-1. It's an ambitious step into model development for Microsoft, which introduced its initial in-house models last year - before then, it had relied on OpenAI's models. The two companies recently renegotiated their deal to loosen ties. According […]

items.title: Microsoft Build 2026: The 7 biggest announcements
items.raw_content: Microsoft just kicked off Build 2026 with a keynote from CEO Satya Nadella and other company leaders. As expected, it was filled with announcements, ranging from new Surface hardware to an always-on personal assistant and updates across Microsoft's in-house AI models. If you didn't watch the event live, you can catch up on all the […]

items.title: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
items.raw_content: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Tuesday creating a "voluntary framework" for AI companies to share their frontier models with the federal government before they're released "to promote secure innovation and strengthen the cybersecurity of critical infrastructure." The order says the US AI industry has succeeded in part "because we refuse to stifle this […]

items.title: Microsoft Scout is a new AI personal assistant built on OpenClaw
items.raw_content: Much like Google, Microsoft is launching its own version of OpenClaw. Microsoft Scout is an always-on assistant that integrates into Microsoft 365 apps like Outlook, OneDrive, and Microsoft Teams, allowing businesses to assign a virtual assistant to employees to help with organizing calendars, expense reporting, email drafts, and much more. Unlike Copilot that lives inside […]

items.title: Google’s Phone app will tell you if a scammer is impersonating one of your contacts
items.raw_content: Google is launching a new feature for its Phone app that aims to protect you from AI impersonation scams. items.title: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
items.raw_content: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.
items.summary: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.

items.title: Cyera eyes $12B valuation at 80x ARR multiple despite operating losses
items.raw_content: The cybersecurity company is nearing a $300 million round led by Evolution Equity Partners.
